Mary Adren

Corporate Lawyer

A Corporate Lawyer specializes in business law and provides legal guidance to companies on a wide range of matters. Their primary role is to ensure that a business complies with all legal and regulatory requirements while helping it achieve its commercial goals. Corporate lawyers draft and review contracts, negotiate deals, manage mergers and acquisitions, and advise on corporate governance, intellectual property, and labor issues.

Our Services

Mergers & Acquisitions

Handling due diligence, negotiations, and regulatory compliance.Structuring and executing asset purchases or stock deals.

Business Formation & Structuring

Advising on the best legal structure Drafting Articles of Incorporation, bylaws, and shareholder agreements.

Corporate Governance

Advising boards of directors and executives on compliance, fiduciary duties, and internal policies,Drafting governance documents and maintaining corporate records

Contract Drafting & Review

Creating, analyzing, and negotiating contracts including vendor agreements, NDAs, employment contracts, and service agreements

Blog

Annual Compliance for Pvt Ltd Companies

Private Limited Companies in India must meet yearly compliance requirements, such as filing financial statements, annual returns, and holding board meetings. Staying compliant avoids penalties and legal issues. This guide helps you understand and manage key compliance deadlines.

Read More
Avtar Singh – Company Law (Eastern Book Company)

Widely used by law students and professionals, this text provides clear explanations of the Companies Act, supplemented with case law and covers the 2013 Act comprehensively.

Read More
Protect Your Brand Legally

Your brand is a valuable asset—don’t leave it vulnerable. Legal protection through trademarks, copyrights, and contracts ensures your brand stays yours. Explore legal strategies to safeguard your identity and build lasting brand equity.

Read More
Which is Right for Your Business Structure?

Choosing the right business structure—Proprietorship, Partnership, LLP, or Pvt Ltd—can affect taxation, liability, and growth potential. Understand the pros and cons of each to make an informed decision that aligns with your goals and industry.

Read More
